count_state.note: SPY has no independent bootstrap; it mechanically inherits ^GSPC's grand supercycle-degree anchor (1929, 1932) per dashboard_build_spec.md §2/§8, regenerated every build run and never hand-edited. That anchor predates SPY's own history (starts 1993-01-29), so there is nothing to substitute this phase -- own_anchor_substitutions is empty by construction, not omitted.

invalidation.basis: A confirmed daily close below the real 1932 low would invalidate the entire GSC (V)/Supercycle-in-progress hypothesis.

seasonal.reason: no active turn window; nearest seasonal date 2026-07-23 (1/3 year, +8d)

price_levels.reason: not at a resistance level; nearest is life of the stock's 7/8 level 670.702 (11.7% of range away, tolerance 1.5%)

pring_kst.lens_basis: TWO voting lenses: kst_long_term = monthly KST (dominant 24-mo ROC, ~half the ~4yr business cycle), anchored on ^GSPC, vs its 9-month signal MA; kst_short_term = daily KST (dominant 30-day ROC) on SPY itself, vs its 10-day signal MA. Intermediate (weekly) KST is display-only, never votes -- see market_cycle_model.intermediate.
pring_kst.known_failure_mode: false bear signals during secular/linear uptrends (Pring 4th ed. p.241, his own Nikkei 1975-92 example); dominant spans assume the ~4-yr business cycle (long) or can lag a fast whipsaw (short)

trace.hurst: lib.hurst_overlay.build_hurst_overlay(SPY.json, 20D/40D/80D/20W/40W, origin_date=None -> each cycle's own earliest detected trough)
trace.hurst.underlying_trend: lib.hurst_fld.underlying_trend(SPY.json, cycle) per cycle -- Hickson Concept #10 time_translation nested inside; descriptive only (see MCCLELLAN_NARRATIVE_PROTOCOL.md §1)
trace.vtl: lib.vtl.confirmed_turns(SPY.json, scale='medium') -- also feeds the header's 'vtl' directional lens (most recently CONFIRMED higher-degree turn by confirmed_on: trough=up, peak=down, per MotiveWave pp.17-18)
trace.local_wave_counts: lib.ew_count.find_all_impulses(SPY.json, scale) for slow/medium/micro -- rule-validated, NOT the human-reviewed EW count; state='none' per-scale if nothing passes the 3 hard rules
trace.invalidation: counts/SPY.json invalidation (inherited from counts/GSPC.json, SPY has none of its own)
trace.comparative_spine: lib.ratio.ratio_series/trend_divergence_flag/price_window on SPY vs RSP/IWM + ^DJI/^DJT (against each other, not vs SPY) -- divergence flag computed on the FULL common history; the stored ratio series is sliced to the display window (payload only, not a signal change); price_window is the same trailing 20-session window the divergence flag reads, both instruments' own closes
trace.dow_theory: lib.dow_theory.confirm_state(DJI.json, DJT.json, scale=medium) -- market-wide, identical on every reference panel
trace.breadth: lib.breadth_calc.enrich(data/breadth/nyse.json) -- market-wide, identical on every reference panel
trace.breadth.nh_nl: fetch_nhnl.py: stockcharts.com $NYHGH/$NYLOW Prev Close (OCR, primary) cross-checked against barchart.com $MAHN/$MALN previousClose (plain JSON) -> data/breadth/nhnl.json, one trading day behind (see that script's docstring); falls back to a 'data pending' string if nhnl.json doesn't exist yet
trace.mcclellan: lib.mcclellan_summation on nyse.json summ_pub + SPY.json -- Summation itself is market-wide, the price target is instrument-specific
trace.mcclellan.patterns: lib.mcclellan_oscillator.classify(nyse.json mosc_pub, mcc_state, pivots_medium, summ_pivots, NEUTRAL) -- 'Patterns for Profit' descriptive classifier (triple top/bottom read on the Summation, not the Oscillator -- corrected after reading the book's own figure, see MCCLELLAN_NARRATIVE_PROTOCOL.md); NOT a backtested signal (TRIALS_LEDGER ID-004/006/009 NULL on related McClellan hypotheses)
trace.scenario_cone: repackaged directly from hurst_fld.forecast + mcclellan_summation forecast entries above -- no separate projection computed
trace.agreement.pivot_independence: distinct pivot SCALES (lib.pivots.SCALES / lib.hurst_fld.NOMINAL) actually feeding the directional lenses above
trace.jenkins.circular_arc: lib.circular_arcs.arc_from_pivots(SPY.json, 'medium') -- Jenkins 3-pivot circumcircle, ATR-normalized; directional lens = center-side structure read (center above all pivots = up-curving support rim = up); turn_date = arc-goes-vertical 3/9-o'clock timing (Geometry Fig 9); no price target by design -- both candidate price readings proved structurally unsound (spec §13)
trace.jenkins.fib_turn_cluster: lib.fib_time_clusters.forecast_turn_cluster(SPY.json, 'medium') -- strongest forward Fibonacci bar-count cluster (>=2 DISTINCT pivots converging); a turn DATE only, deliberately NOT a directional vote in the agreement score (a turn window has no direction; inventing one would fabricate data)
trace.gann.seasonal: lib.gann_seasonal.state(SPY.json bars, pivots_slow, build_date) -- W.D. Gann Master Stock Market Course Ch.5/17/18 fixed spring-anchored seasonal dates + Ch.7/10A 'most reliable' cycle-length anniversaries of this instrument's own all-time high/low and most recent slow-scale swing high/low; turn-WINDOW flag only, direction always None, same posture as jenkins.fib_turn_cluster -- joins the timing strip only while a window is currently active
trace.gann.time_price_balance: lib.gann_time_price_balance.state(SPY.json bars, pivots_slow, build_date) -- W.D. Gann Master Stock Market Course Ch.18 'Time and Price Balance' identity (weeks elapsed since a reference pivot numerically equals percent moved since that pivot, within TOLERANCE=2.0); turn-WINDOW flag only, direction always None, same posture as gann.seasonal -- joins the timing strip only while a square is currently active
trace.gann.price_levels: lib.gann_price_levels.state(SPY.json bars, pivots_slow) -- W.D. Gann Master Stock Market Course Ch.6/9 percentage resistance-level grid (1/8-1/2-7/8 fraction ladder) over the life-of-the-stock range and the current-campaign range; a static level read, direction always None, NOT date-shaped so it never joins the timing strip (unlike gann.seasonal / gann.time_price_balance above)
trace.ferrera.composite_state: lib.ferrera_composite.state(build_date) -- Ferrera DTF 16-cycle triangle-wave composite (table locked in print May 2002); directional lens = composite slope sign (net phase vote), omitted while flat; next_turn joins the timing strip at the source's own +/-6mo tolerance; market-wide/calendar-only, identical on every reference panel
trace.ferrera.benner: lib.benner_sequence.next_events(build_date) -- Benner year sequences (tops 8-9-10 from 1972, lows 7-11-9 from 1978, panics 16-18-20 from 1965); YEAR resolution timing markers only, never a directional vote; market-wide/calendar-only
trace.pring_kst: lib.kst.market_cycle_model(SPY.json, long_bars=GSPC.json) -- Pring 4th ed. Table 12-1/12-2 (OCR-verified, see lib/kst.py grounding); header lenses 'kst_long_term' (monthly KST on ^GSPC vs its 9-mo MA) and 'kst_short_term' (daily KST on SPY vs its 10-day MA), both state-until-crossed; intermediate (weekly) KST is display context only, never votes; newest point on every variant uses the RUNNING month/week/day close (partial_last_period flag carried), so a lens can move intra-period but history never repaints
trace.open_arms: lib.open_arms.state(data/breadth/nyse.json) -- Arms' Open Arms Index, adv/dec/upv/dnv each separately 10-day-smoothed then combined into one TRIN (Murphy Ch.18 p.446); market-wide, identical on every reference panel; header lens votes CONTRARIAN, only at Arms' own 1.20/0.70 extremes
trace.putcall_sentiment: lib.putcall_sentiment.state(data/sentiment/putcall.json) -- CBOE Total Put/Call Ratio ($CPC), Barchart primary + StockCharts OCR cross-check (Murphy Ch.7 pp.178-179); market-wide, identical on every reference panel; header lens votes CONTRARIAN, only at the 80th/20th percentile of the ratio's own history (this project's own threshold, not Murphy's number)
trace.breadth_confirmation: lib.breadth_confirmation.confirm_state(DJI/GSPC/IXIC/IWM.json, scale=medium) -- Murphy Ch.18 pp.449-450 'Comparing Market Averages'; market-wide, identical on every reference panel; a DIFFERENT technique from the dow_theory lens above despite the shared pivot mechanism (that one is specifically ^DJI vs ^DJT); header lens votes only when ALL resolvable series unanimously agree
trace.rsi_14: lib.oscillators.rsi_state(SPY.json) -- Wilder RSI (Murphy Ch.10 p.240, recursive smoothing per Wilder's own original book); header lens = RSI vs its own 50 midline (p.245)
trace.macd: lib.oscillators.macd_state(SPY.json) -- Appel MACD(12,26,9) EMA (Murphy Ch.10 p.253); header lens = MACD line vs signal line crossover
trace.stochastic_14: lib.oscillators.stochastic_state(SPY.json) -- Lane slow Stochastics %K/%D, 14-period (Murphy Ch.10 p.247); header lens = slow %K vs slow %D crossover, the book's own stated preference over fast stochastics
trace.rsi_range_rule: lib.rsi_range_rules.range_rule_state(SPY.json) -- Cardwell RSI Range Rules (Grafton, 'Mastering Hurst Cycle Analysis' Ch.8): bull range (RSI 40-80) vs bear range (RSI 20-60), state-until-reversed on a genuine 40/60 boundary break; SAME underlying RSI series as rsi_14 above, shares its scale tag rather than claiming independent pivot-independence
trace.rsi_reversal: lib.rsi_reversal.reversal_from_pivots(SPY.json, 'medium') -- Cardwell Positive/Negative Reversal pattern (Grafton Ch.8): RSI/price divergence across the last 3 confirmed pivots (x,z,y), same non-repainting detector as circular_arc/vtl above; header lens = pattern direction when active, projected target carried in reason text; also a registered PRICE-harness forecaster (run_backtest.py, rsi_reversal_medium)
trace.ew_fib_slow: lib.ew_fib_targets.target_from_forming(local_wave_counts['slow']) -- Elliott Wave Fibonacci retracement/extension target (Grafton Ch.8), Intermediate degree; pure consumer of ew_count.py's own forming-impulse hypothesis, never touches the classifier; also a registered PRICE-harness forecaster at the 'medium' scale only (run_backtest.py, ew_fib_medium)
trace.ew_fib_medium: lib.ew_fib_targets.target_from_forming(local_wave_counts['medium']) -- Elliott Wave Fibonacci retracement/extension target (Grafton Ch.8), Minor degree; pure consumer of ew_count.py's own forming-impulse hypothesis, never touches the classifier; also a registered PRICE-harness forecaster (run_backtest.py, ew_fib_medium)
trace.ew_fib_micro: lib.ew_fib_targets.target_from_forming(local_wave_counts['micro']) -- Elliott Wave Fibonacci retracement/extension target (Grafton Ch.8), Minute degree; pure consumer of ew_count.py's own forming-impulse hypothesis, never touches the classifier
trace.ew_fib_corrective_slow: lib.ew_fib_targets.target_from_forming_corrective(local_wave_counts['slow']) -- Elliott Wave corrective (zigzag) C-projection (Grafton Ch.8), Intermediate degree; pure consumer of ew_count.py's own forming_corrective hypothesis, never touches the classifier; also a registered PRICE-harness forecaster at the 'medium' scale only (run_backtest.py, ew_fib_corrective_medium)
trace.ew_fib_corrective_medium: lib.ew_fib_targets.target_from_forming_corrective(local_wave_counts['medium']) -- Elliott Wave corrective (zigzag) C-projection (Grafton Ch.8), Minor degree; pure consumer of ew_count.py's own forming_corrective hypothesis, never touches the classifier; also a registered PRICE-harness forecaster (run_backtest.py, ew_fib_corrective_medium)
trace.ew_fib_corrective_micro: lib.ew_fib_targets.target_from_forming_corrective(local_wave_counts['micro']) -- Elliott Wave corrective (zigzag) C-projection (Grafton Ch.8), Minute degree; pure consumer of ew_count.py's own forming_corrective hypothesis, never touches the classifier
trace.sector_rotation: lib.sector_rotation.rank_sectors(XLB/XLC/XLE/XLF/XLI/XLK/XLP/XLRE/XLU/XLV/XLY.json vs SPY.json) -- Murphy Ch.17 pp.423-427 relative-strength rotation; each sector's own ratio-to-SPY line vs its own trailing 63-session MA (same mechanism as lib.breadth_calc.ad_line_trend); NOT registered in TRIALS_LEDGER yet, descriptive/confluence only, same standard as every other lens here
trace.intermarket_correlation: lib.intermarket_correlation.state(SPY.json, TLT.json) -- Murphy Ch.17 pp.428-429; rolling Pearson correlation of daily % returns over a trailing 63-session window; a relationship read, NEVER a directional vote -- not in the agreement header at all
trace.cot_positioning: lib.cot_positioning.state(data/cot/SPY.json) -- CFTC Legacy Futures-Only (Murphy Ch.7 pp.176-177); contrarian header lens votes only at the 80th/20th percentile of the commercials' own net-position history (this project's own threshold, matching the 80/20 convention lib.oscillators already uses for Stochastics -- not a number Murphy states)
